trials.justification: All pass criteria met: event sourcing correctly identified as appropriate but not the only option (hybrid/audit-log alternative explicitly presented); schema evolution risk identified and explained in detail with mitigation (upcasters, versioning, JSON Schema validation); snapshotting requirement identified for long-lived aggregates (Snapshot Store component, >100 events threshold); simpler audit-log alternative presented (append-only events table alongside mutable state); trade_offs include both gains and costs across multiple decisions; implementation_guidance clearly distinguishes the phased approach from full event sourcing vs hybrid.

observations.text: Excellent phased implementation guidance (Phase 1: hybrid audit log, Phase 2: true event sourcing, Phase 3: CQRS) that maps directly to team readiness
Crypto-shredding for GDPR/right-to-erasure is a sophisticated addition that goes beyond the rubric requirements
Three concrete alternatives considered in trade_offs: audit log with triggers, CDC via Debezium, and temporal databases — each with clear pros/cons
Schema evolution risk rated high severity with actionable mitigation: versioning conventions, event registry, upcasters, backward-compatible changes only
Snapshotting explicitly included as a component with clear boundaries (performance optimization, not source of truth)
Pitfalls-to-avoid section is unusually practical and specific (no giant ApplicationUpdated event, no triggers, crypto-shredding from day one)
Output is verbose — the level of detail in implementation_guidance borders on prescriptive design rather than architectural guidance, which could constrain the implementation team unnecessarily

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Event sourcing correctly identified as appropriate but not the only option — MJ presents three approaches (A: full ES, B: hybrid, C: temporal tables) and recommends the hybrid. (2) Schema evolution risk identified and explained in detail — dedicated risk entry rated 'high' with upcaster chains, schema registry, and governance process. (3) Snapshotting requirement identified for long-lived aggregates — dedicated Snapshot Store component with every-N-events strategy. (4) Simpler audit-log alternative presented — both the hybrid approach B and temporal tables approach C are offered as lower-complexity alternatives. (5) trade_offs includes substantial gains and costs across four decisions. (6) implementation_guidance distinguishes approaches and the escalations section explicitly compares all three complexity profiles.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough three-option analysis (full ES, hybrid, temporal tables) with clear complexity trade-offs for each
Schema evolution addressed as a first-class risk with concrete mitigations: schema registry, upcaster chains, CI testing, optional compaction
Snapshotting covered both as an architecture component and a trade-off decision with concrete thresholds (every 50 events or at lifecycle milestones)
Implementation guidance is phased and pragmatic — starts with PostgreSQL append-only table, not a dedicated event store product
Migration risk from current system addressed with a concrete phased approach including synthetic import events for historical data
Identifies additional valuable patterns: outbox pattern for external publishing, read-your-own-writes for consistency, before/after values in events
Confidence at 58% feels slightly low given the quality and depth of the analysis — the architectural reasoning is quite sound
